title: Serious Pathology Red Flags for Physio
synonyms: Serious Pathology Red Flags for Physio

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: This guideline summarises the situations where a physiotherapy or rehab presentation should stop being routine conservative care and become urgent medical escalation.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation
sections.content:
- Unexplained fever, unexplained weight loss, or systemic decline
- Previous history of cancer — any new or changed pain in a patient with a cancer history is serious pathology until proven otherwise
- Immunosuppression: HIV, chemotherapy, organ transplant, or prolonged corticosteroid use — these increase the risk of spinal infection, epidural abscess, and atypical fracture
- Intravenous drug use — significantly increases the risk of septic arthritis, osteomyelitis, and spinal infection
- Progressive neurological deficit, new gait change, or new bladder or bowel symptoms
- Major trauma, deformity, or suspected fracture
- Unrelenting night pain, severe unexplained pain, or pain out of proportion
- Chest pain, breathlessness, syncope, calf swelling, or other systemic red flags during an apparent MSK review

sections.title: Considerations
sections.content:
- Red flags are prompts for clinical judgement, not isolated tick-boxes
- Change from the patient's normal pattern is often as important as any single symptom
- The physio role includes recognizing when the problem is outside physio scope, even if the patient primarily booked for rehab or work assessment

sections.title: Management - Primary
sections.content:
- Pause routine treatment when red flags are identified
- Recheck vitals and clarify the timeline of concerning symptoms
- Escalate to doctor, emergency department, or ambulance response according to the severity and environment

sections.title: Management - Advanced
sections.content:
- Communicate clearly what is concerning and why conservative care is not appropriate today
- Document the red flags screened, positive findings, and the escalation advice or transfer pathway used
- Use this guideline alongside region-specific pathways rather than as a replacement for them

sections.title: Additional Notes
sections.content: - Good physio escalation protects the patient and the clinician; it is not a failure of conservative care
